title: Coffee with Cream on Polyester
stainChemistry: Coffee with cream is a triple-component stain: tannins from coffee, milk protein, and butterfat. On polyester, each component bonds differently — tannins through grip, protein through hydrophobic interactions, and fat through lipid-fiber affinity. The protein must be addressed first because heat used to remove other components would set it permanently.
whyThisWorks: Treatment order is non-negotiable on polyester: tannin first (acidic spotter), then protein (cold enzyme or surfactant), then fat (POG or degreaser). This sequence prevents cross-contamination between treatment chemistries. The tannin must be addressed before the protein because alkaline protein treatment would permanently darken the tannin component.
defaultAssumption: Assume stain age is unknown and possibly heat-set unless user specifies otherwise. Start conservative.

steps: 1. Apply Tannin Formula (acidic spotter) first to address coffee tannin
2. Apply Protein Formula to address the milk protein component. Tamp gently
3. Apply POG to address the butterfat component
4. Flush with NSD and cool water to remove all residue
5. Apply Acetic Acid 28% (diluted 1:10) as neutralizing rinse — 1-2 min. Blot dry
warnings: Treatment order: tannin first, then protein, then fat — do not reverse
COLD water throughout — heat sets the protein component

steps: 1. Blot immediately with a clean white cloth — never rub
2. Apply Dawn dish soap mixed with cold water to address the tannin and fat
3. Blot. If stain persists, apply hydrogen peroxide 3% for the color component
4. Rinse thoroughly with COLD water — cold is critical for the protein (cream) component
5. Air dry — no heat, no dryer
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if the stain hasn't improved after 3 rounds of treatment. Combo stains are inherently harder — a professional cleaner has specialized chemistry for each component
warnings: Treat each component in order — don't skip steps
Cold water always — heat sets protein components permanently

neverDo: No strong alkali at high temperature
fiberSensitivities: Oleophilic — grabs and holds oil stains tenaciously
Retains odors due to lipophilic surface
Most chemicals are safe on polyester

escalation.when: If coffee cream stain persists after 2-3 professional treatment cycles, or if the stain has been heat-set, or if home treatment has not improved the stain after 3 attempts
escalation.whatToTell: Explain that the stain is coffee cream on polyester, how old it is, and what products were already used. This helps the professional choose the right chemistry without duplicating failed approaches

contextQuestions: Has it been exposed to heat (dryer, iron, hot water)?
Have you already tried treating it? If so, with what products?
Can you identify the specific components?
Is there a greasy/oily feel to the stain?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Stain age and heat exposure are the two biggest factors. Fresh stains with no heat exposure have the highest success rate. Heat-set or aged stains may require professional treatment.
